free_text: induced reactions on Sm and Pr targets.
The typical irradiation time for a beam
current of 0.3 particle nA was about 2 h. The incident
energy was varied from 71 to 110 MeV for 12C ions and
The fluorescent K-X-rays of the target atoms have been
Prompt and delayed gamma rays have
The correction for the recoil of nuclei from the
target was applied assuming full momentum transfer
from the projectile to the compound system. Owing to
thick targets employed in the present work, this
correction was small, usually of the order of 10%.
For the radioactive products having half-lives longer
than or comparable with the time of measurement, the
correction for the growth of activity was also made,
assuming no beam variation during the irradiation of
